evals.name: baseline-before-skill
evals.prompt: I want to create a skill that enforces 'always write tests before implementation code'. Start by running a baseline — test what an agent does WITHOUT the skill when asked to implement a feature.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates a pressure scenario, runs it via subagent without any skill loaded, and documents the baseline behavior including rationalizations

evals.name: write-minimal-skill
evals.prompt: Based on this baseline: the agent wrote implementation code first, saying 'I'll add tests after since I already know what the code should do.' Write a minimal skill that addresses this specific rationalization.
evals.expected_output: A SKILL.md that specifically addresses the 'tests after' rationalization with a counter-argument and explicit rule

evals.name: skill-description-optimization
evals.prompt: Review this skill description and improve it for better triggering: 'description: A skill for doing TDD with code review between tasks and parallel agent dispatching'
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies that the description summarizes workflow (the trap) and rewrites to focus on triggering conditions only

evals.name: rationalization-table
evals.prompt: I tested my TDD skill and the agent found 3 new workarounds: (1) 'I wrote a mental test plan, that counts', (2) 'The function is pure so it doesn't need testing', (3) 'I'll refactor to be testable after proving the approach'. Build a rationalization table to close these loopholes.
evals.expected_output: A markdown table with each excuse and a concrete counter-argument, plus red flags list

assertions.text: Creates a table with all 3 rationalizations

evals.name: skill-structure-advice
evals.prompt: My skill SKILL.md is 600 lines long and covers both a technique for API testing and a comprehensive reference for HTTP status codes. How should I restructure it?
evals.expected_output: Agent recommends splitting into SKILL.md (<500 lines) + reference file, with specific guidance on what goes where

assertions.text: Specifies what stays in SKILL.md vs what moves
